generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T TILIA. Tilia Europæa, Linn. Natural order , Tiliaceæ. Common names , Linden, Linde. Preparation , Tincture of the fresh blossoms. Authorities. Austrian provings, st. Zeit. für Hom., vol. 4, part 2, p. 380, provings made at different seasons of the year; 1 , E. H. Froelich, took an infusion of 2 drachms of the dried flowers; 1 a , same, took on two successive days 3 drops of 1st dec. dil., aferwards 20 drops; 1 b , same, took 6th dec. dil. in water; 2 , Th. K., took 3d dec. dil.; 2 a , same, took 3 drops of 6th dec. dil. (first and second days), and at subsequent times; 3 , F. G., took the tincture prepared from the fresh blossoms, 10 drops, afterwards 20 drops; 4 , J. O. Müller, took 10 drops of the tincture in water; 4 a , same, took 10 drops of 1st dec. dil., afterwards same dose, subsequently 20 drops; 4 b , same, took 10 drops of 3d dec. dil., twice, subsequently 60 drops; 4 c , same, took 10, then 20 drops of 6th dec. dil.; 4 d , same, took 10, afterwards 20 drops of 3th dil.; 5 , K. P., took 10 drops 1st dec. dil.; 5 a , same, took 3 drops of 3d dil.; 6 , E. K., a child, took 1 drop of 1st dec. dil. in water; 7 , B. S., took 10, and afterwards 20 drops 1st dec. dil.; 7 a , same, took 60 drops 3d dec. dil.; 7 b , same, took 20 drops of 6th dec. dil.; 7 c , same, took 20 drops of 30th dil. (first day), 10 drops (third day), 20 drops afterwards. MIND. ► Towards morning she was tormented by a rush of pleasant thoughts, which changed to a weeping mood; through the next day she was peculiarly irritable, and inclined to quarrel, with confusion of the head, with a tense accelerated pulse and increased warmth of the body, especially of the cheeks, [2a] . ► Lovesick; all his thoughts centred upon an ideal woman; in this revery he was possessed by a sweet melancholy, which it was impossible to describe; every earthly sense seemed far away, [3] . ► Earnest, reflective mood (first day), [4d] . ► Sad, weeping mood (first day), [7] . ► Sad, melancholy mood (second day), [4a] . ► Apprehensive mood (second day), [4b] . ► He cannot remain in the house on account of a sensation of apprehension and anxiety; the room seems too close, he is obliged to go into the open air, in the evening, when he feels better (first day), [4d] . ► Extremely depressed after dinner (second day), [4a] . ► Great depression of spirits the whole forenoon (third day), [4a] . [10.] ► Despondent (first day), [4a] . ► Despondency after dinner (second day), [4d] . ► Depression of spirits, [3] , [4a] , [4b] . ► Dread of society (second day), [4a] . ► Discontented with his condition (first day), [4a] . ► Unusually fretful mood (second day), [3] . ► Fretful, quarrelsome, peevish (third day), [4a] . ► Irritable mood, inclined to get angry, in the morning (second day), [1] . ► Irritable, quarrelsome mood after dinner (third day), [4d] . ► Irritable, critical mood, inclined to quarrel and get angry, even from the slightest difference of opinion (first day), [4d] . [20.] ► Disinclination to work (fourth day), [7c] . ► Thoughtfulness (second day), [4d] . ► Stupidity, [2a] , [4b] . ► An intoxicated and stupid condition, with mental oppression (second day), [4] . HEAD. ► Confusion and Vertigo. ► Confusion of the head, which seemed too large, in the morning (second day), [1] . ► Dizzy confusion of the head, with dimness of vision (after a few hours), [4a] . ► Dizzy confusion of the head, with slight drawing pain in the right half of the forehead (third day), [4a] . ► Confusion, heaviness, and obscuration of the head, so that he could think and speak clearly only with great effort (third day), [4b] . ► Violent dizzy confusion of the head, with pressure on the chest, [4c] . ► Confusion of the head, and so great dizziness that he feared he would lose his consciousness, [4c] . [30.] ► Dizzy, intoxicated confusion of the head, with heat of the face, with redness of the cheeks, soon, [4d] . ► Persistent intoxication of the head, with tearing drawing or sticking pains, here and there, [7b] . ► Intoxication and nausea in the stomach, [4c] . ► A feeling of intoxication in the head (first day), [4a] . ► Vertigo, as if intoxicated, during which she seemed to turn in a circle, so that she was obliged to sit down (soon), [7] . ► Dizziness, [4c] . ► Vertigo, all objects seem to waver (third day), [4b] . ► Paroxysms of vertigo, with heat of the face, [4c] . ► Vertigo, with dimness of vision by paroxysms, [7c] . ► Vertigo, with such great exhaustion that he was obliged to take hold of objects to steady himself (second day), [4b] . [40.] ► Transient vertigo, with vanishing of vision, in the evening; in order to see distinctly she was obliged to wink repeatedly, in the evening (first day), [7c] . ► Transient vertigo and obscuration of the head, [3] . ► Slight vertigo while walking in the open air, with obscuration of the eyes (soon), [1b] . ► She seems to lose the freedom and steadiness of her movements, and staggers on attempting to stand up; as the vertigo passes off a painful dulness of the head comes on (first day), [7] . ► General Head. ► Dulness of the head, [1a] , [4c] , [4d] . ► Dulness of the head, the whole forenoon (second day), [1] . ► Dulness of the head in the morning, increased by the warmth of the room to a pressive pain (third day), [1] . ► Dulness of the head, in the morning (third day), [2] . ► Painful dulness of the head, at times in the occiput, at times in the forehead (first day), [4d] . ► Dizzy, confused dulness of the head at 10 A.M. (third day), [4d] . [50.] ► Painful dulness of the head, [7c] . ► Fulness and heaviness of the head (third day), [1] . ► Stupefaction and painful dulness of the head, after dinner (second day), [4a] . ► Heaviness of the head, [8] . ► Heaviness at night, and dulness of the head (first day), [4a] . ► Heaviness and dulness of the head at times (third day), [4a] . ► Headache increased in the evening, with a dull confusion in the frontal region, stoppage of the nose, and roaring in the ears (second day), [3] . ► The head feels heavy, and as if screwed in, [4c] . ► The head felt hot and heavy, on waking at night (first night), [4] . ► Woke about 5 P.M., from a sleep of three hours, free from the morning headache, with the exception of some fulness in the forehead, and slight dizziness and obscuration of vision (second day), [1] . [60.] ► Headache, accompanied by heat of the face, lasting through the night, till noon of the next day, [2a] . ► Painful pressive-drawing pains in the head, extending towards the forehead (first day), [4b] . ► Violent drawing-cutting pain in the head, with photophobia and qualmishness, aggravated by the slightest movement, intolerable on making any effort, relieved by closing the eyes, and by cold applications, and by rest (third day), [4b] . ► Tearing pains through the brain (first day), [4a] . ► Tearing pains, here and there, in the head, face, and ears, [3] . ► Cutting drawing, here and there, in the left half of the brain towards the forehead (second day), [4a] . ► Violent sticking pain over the whole head, with heat of the whole body, [2a] . ► Extreme sensitiveness of the head to a draught of air; even while walking in the house the current of air caused slight drawing and shivering in the scalp on the vertex, [4a] . ► Amelioration of the head troubles from coffee (third day), [1] . ► Forehead. ► The forehead feels obscured, as if constricted (third day), [4d] . [70.] ► Pressure in the forehead, [3] ; (third day), [4d] . ► Pressive pain in the frontal region (second day), [1] . ► Painful pressure in the forehead, soon, [4d] . ► Pressive pinching pain in the sinciput (forehead and vertex), in the evening; this continued into the night, and was felt whenever awakened from sleep, by restless dreams (first night), [3] . ► Pain in the forehead, just above the root of the nose, a pressing inward, as from a dull instrument (first day), [4d] . ► Slow wavelike drawings in the left half of the forehead, extending towards the frontal eminence (second day), [4b] . ► Fulness and tearing in the forehead, lasting till evening, then associated with great heat of the face, [4c] . ► Tearing pain in the forehead, with dizziness in the morning on waking, increasing after rising, relieved by washing with cold water, [4c] . ► Dull sticking burrowing in the right frontal eminence (second day), [4b] . ► Tearing, sticking pain in the right side of the forehead, [4c] . ► Temples. [80.] ► Temporal veins very much distended (second day), [4d] . ► Pressive pain in the temples, worse in a warm room, better in the open air; this headache reached its height about 1 o'clock; the pressure in the temples amounted to an acute pressing pain; the whole head was painful, heavy, dull, and confused (second day), [1] . ► Drawing in the left temple and side of the face (first day), [4a] . ► Transient tearing pain in the right temple, [7b] . ► Tearing in the right temple, and at the same time extending down the upper arm (first day), [7] . ► Sticking pains from without inward, in both temples (first day), [7] . ► Vertex and Parietals. ► Tearing in the right side of the vertex, transversely across the forehead, and along the left side of the face (first day), [7] . ► Painful drawing in the left half of the head, rather towards the occiput, and down towards the face (second day), [4a] . ► Occiput. ► Heaviness of the occiput; it seemed as though a weight were seated in it; when the head is inclined forward this weight seems to sink lower down in the nape of the neck (first day), [7] . ► A peculiar aching pain in the occiput, extending up towards the back of the head, after dinner (first day), [4d] . [90.] ► Pressure in the occiput from within outward, at the mastoid process (second day), [4a] . ► Broad pressure in the occiput, [4c] . ► Pressure and burrowing pain in the occiput, [4c] . ► Drawing pain in the fold between the occiput and the nape of the neck (first day), [4a] . ► Drawing in the occiput into the head and to the forehead, where it becomes a pressure, [4c] . ► External Head. ► Eruption of small gritty vesicles on the scalp, which itch excessively, with burning soreness after scratching (first day), [4a] . ► Itching on the scalp, obliging scratching (first day), [4a] . ► Itching on the scalp (second day), [4a] . EYE. ► Eyes deep, hollow, [7b] . ► Eyes drawn backward (hollow-eyed), (third day), [4d] . [100.] ► Eyes thick and weak, seem swollen, [4c] . ► The eyes seem swollen and weak, as if one had been weeping, [7b] . ► Pressive pain in both eyes (second day), [1] . ► A transient sensation, as if a piece of cold iron pierced through the right eye, causing burning (first day), [7] . ► Stitches upon the outer portion of the eye, afterwards in them, [7c] . ► Itching about the eye, [7a] . ► Lids. ► Itching of the internal canthi, with weakness of sight (first day), [4b] . ► Lachrymation. ► Lachrymation (third day), [7c] . ► Vision. ► Vision obscured, [4c] . ► Paroxysms of dimness of vision, as if a mist were hanging before the eyes; in the afternoon these attacks recurred, the eyes seemed "thick and weak," as after a long sleep; it was an effort to see distinctly, and she was obliged to open the eyes as widely as possible (first day), [7a] . [110.] ► Dimness of vision, as from a veil (second day), [3] . ► Mist before the eyes (soon), [7] . ► Vision weak (third day), [4d] . ► Vision very weak, obscured (first day), [4d] . ► Weakness of sight, with obscuration of vision, soon, [4d] . ► Swimming of sight, with moist eyes, [4c] . ► The candlelight seems enveloped in smoke or vapor; the eyes are weak and tense, as after long weeping, or as if the white of an egg had dried upon them, [4c] . ► Flickering of the candlelight in the evening; all objects seemed to waver, and she was obliged to make great effort in order to see distinctly; when she looked with only one eye the vision was perfect; with both eyes objects seemed indistinct, and wavering; this disturbance of vision increased with the evening, so that at last small objects could not be seen at all, [7b] . EAR. ► Tearing around the outer portion of the ear in the root of the concha, as if it would be torn out, in repeated paroxysms (fourth day), [4d] . ► Tearing in the margin of the right ear, as if the concha were being torn off, [4c] . [120.] ► Transient burning sensation, as if a piece of ice were drawn over the ear and face (first day), [7] . ► Painful sensation of distension in the inner ear (fourth day), [4d] . ► Drawing in the left ear, as after taking cold (first day), [4a] . ► Heat and tearing, extending from within outward, in the right ear, [4c] . ► Tearing from within outward, in the left ear, with tearing in the left side of the head (second day), [4a] . ► Tickling in the right ear, as if something were running from it, [4c] . ► Sticking pain in the ears (second day), [4b] . ► Tearing and stopped sensation in the ears (second day), [3] . ► Hearing. ► Ringing or dull roaring in the ears, [3] . ► Transient ringing in the ears (third day), [3] . NOSE. [130.] ► Sneezing (second day), [4d] . ► Frequent sneezing with chilliness (second day), [4a] . ► Frequent sneezing through the day (third day), [3] . ► Much sneezing, with fluent coryza and scraping in the throat, [7c] . ► At night he woke from a partial slumber, with sneezing, fluent coryza, and stoppage of one nostril (second night), [3] . ► Clear thin blood from both nostrils, in the evening (third day), [3] . ► Stopped sensation in the nose, which was dry (third day), [2] . ► Drawing pain in the root of the nose, [4c] . ► Burrowing pain within the nose, [4c] . ► Transient tearing pain in the nose, [7b] . [140.] ► Tickling in the nose (second day), [4d] . FACE. ► Face sickly, hollow-eyed (second day), [4b] . ► Redness of the face, [2a] . ► Paleness of the face (second day), [4b] . ► Frequent alternations in the color of the face (second day), [6] . ► Constrictive pain in the face, beginning in the left malar bone (first day), [4b] . ► Extremely exhausting, ulcerative, or sore pain in the right side of the face, corresponding to the malar bone, afterwards also in the left side of the face, beginning in the temple and extending down along the cheek to the lower jaw, and ending in the gum (third day), [7c] . ► Cutting pain in the face, starting from the right upper jaw, and recurring in paroxysms (third day), [4d] . ► Sensation as if something living were under the skin of the face, [7b] . ► Cheek. ► Cutting-drawing pain in the malar bone, beneath the left eye, [4c] . [150.] ► Cutting constrictive pain in the face near the left malar bone, [4c] . ► Lips. ► Quivering of the lips (first day), [4a] . ► Itching biting of the lower lip (third day), [7c] . ► Chin. ► Pain in the left articulation of the jaw, as if sprained (second day), [5a] . ► Painful cramp in the left articulation of the jaw, hindering chewing (third day), [5] . ► Drawing along the whole right side of the jaws, with heat of the cheeks, [4c] . ► Burning drawing along the left lower jaw, [4c] . ► Tearing pain and sensation of heat in the left lower jaw, caused or aggravated by cold water, [3] . MOUTH. ► Teeth. ► Painful drawing pains in the teeth of both jaws, with a sensitiveness to cold water (first day), [7] . ► Tearing pain in the teeth, more in the alveolar processes of the jaw (third day), [7c] . [160.] ► Burning pain in the right upper back teeth, and afterwards in the corresponding teeth of the left jaw (after three hours), [4b] . ► Gums. ► Drawing pains in the gums (first day), [4b] . ► General Mouth. ► Much mucus in the mouth on waking in the morning (third day), [4b] . ► The whole mouth is coated with mucus, which adheres to the teeth and causes a fuzzy feeling in the mouth, decidedly affecting her speech, in the morning after waking (second day), [5] . ► The breath comes hot from the mouth (first day), [4a] . ► Blisters in the right corner of the mouth, [7b] . ► The roof of the mouth and tongue seemed covered with tallow, a fuzzy feeling, which could not be removed by frequent empty swallowing (first day), [7a] . ► Scraping tickling in the palate and isthmus of the fauces, provoking a dry hacking cough (second day), [4d] . ► Scraping on the palate, in the forenoon (second day), [4d] . ► Sensation of constriction on the inner surface of the cheeks, as from acids or astringents (first day), [4b] . ► Taste. [170.] ► Flat, slimy, sweetish taste in the mouth, in the morning (second day), [4] . ► Sour, slimy taste in the mouth, after waking (first day), [4a] . ► A sweetish flat taste in the mouth (second day), [4b] . ► Bitter taste in the morning (third day), [4b] . ► Slimy, bitter taste in the mouth (third day), [4d] . ► Slimy, pasty taste in the mouth, with a white-coated tongue, in the morning (second day), [7c] . ► Loss of relish for the customary smoke (second day), [1] . THROAT. ► Rawness of the throat, and a feeling as if the uvula were elongated, with desire to swallow (soon), [4a] . ► Rawness of the throat, with paroxysms of hoarseness and mucus in the throat (first day), [4a] . ► Scraping in the throat, [4c] . [180.] ► Slight burning in the throat (soon), [4b] . ► Burning at the entrance of the fauces (soon), [4a] . STOMACH. ► Appetite and Thirst. ► Unusual hunger (second day), [4a] . ► Unusually good appetite, at noon (third day), [4a] . ► Longing for different things, which, when eaten, cause nausea (first day), [4a] . ► Desire for refreshing things (third day), [4a] . ► He sat down to dinner with hunger and good appetite, but was speedily satisfied; after which, all his food nauseated him; even the least food caused great pressure in the stomach, [4a] . ► He went to dinner with great hunger, yet he did not have his usual appetite, and food seemed to have an acrid taste, as though the palate and fauces were sore; also, he was so completely satisfied by eating a little that he was somewhat nauseated (third day), [4d] . ► Desire to eat, but no appetite; he was averse to food, and was satisfied by a few morsels (second day), [4] . ► Appetite greatly diminished (third day), [4] . [190.] ► Good appetite, but speedy satiety, with nausea after eating (second day), [4a] . ► Loss of appetite (second day), [3] , [6] ; (third day), [7c] . ► Very little desire to smoke, in the evening (first day), [4a] . ► No desire for tobacco; when he attempts to use it, it disgusts him (first day), [4a] . ► Thirst (second day), [4a] . ► Eructations and Hiccough. ► Foul eructations without relief (fourth day), [7c] . ► Foul eructations, tasting of Sulphuretted hydrogen (first day), [4d] . ► Numerous eructations, at times spasmodic, with a loud sound, [3] . ► Eructations tasting like the juice of Thuja, although he had taken none (first day), [4a] . ► Frequently repeated eructations of air, soon, [4] . [200.] ► Frequent eructations of air, followed by hiccough (first day), [7] . ► Spasmodic hiccough-like eructations (soon), [4b] . ► Nausea. ► Nausea (first day), [4a] ; (second day), [4b] . ► Nausea after eating (second day), [3] . ► The thought of food causes nausea, in the evening (first day), [4a] . ► Nausea, with accumulation of water in the mouth (second day), [4a] . ► Nausea and slight inclination to vomit (third day), [4b] . ► Nausea, weariness, and prostration in the limbs, all day (second day), [5a] . ► Nausea immediately after eating, though she ate little and with good appetite (first day), [7] . ► Nausea, with dizziness of the head, as if it would sink down, [4c] . [210.] ► Some nausea, [4c] . ► Stomach. ► Swelling of the epigastric region, which was very sensitive to touch (second day), [4a] . ► Feeling of swelling and soreness extending from the stomach to the throat; on swallowing the food seemed to remain sticking in the throat; on coughing, the parts were sensitive; afterwards, the pain extended transversely from the stomach to the back; the sensation of soreness was especially noticed on coughing and sneezing (first day), [7] . ► Painful distension of the epigastric region (third day), [4b] . ► Spasmodic gnawing pain in the stomach, like hunger, with much yawning, before breakfast; after eating there was relief, but the pain returned and lasted the whole forenoon (second day), [4b] . ► The stomach feels overloaded after a light dinner, with nausea, increasing to efforts to vomit, lasting several days, [3] . ► Distress in the stomach, as if overloaded, [4] . ► Great faintness at the stomach (first day), [4a] . ► A very faintlike pain in the stomach (second day), [4d] . ► Violent pain in the stomach, in the morning, fasting, [4c] . [220.] ► Pain in the stomach, with slight nausea, which compelled her to eat, after which she felt better (first day), [5] . ► Pain in the stomach, with accumulation of much saliva in the mouth, [4c] . ► Violent cramp in the stomach after eating salad (second day), [4a] . ► Cramp in the stomach, in the morning on waking (third day), [4a] . ► Cramplike pain in the stomach a few hours after dinner (second day), [4b] . ► Pressure in the pit of the stomach (first day), [4a] . ► Severe pressure in the stomach (fourth day), [7c] . ► A sudden, only gradually disappearing, stitch in the pit of the stomach, [4c] . ► A sticking-pinching pain in the stomach and right hypochondrium, only when walking (after one hour), [4a] . ► Violent burning in the stomach (first day), [4a] . ABDOMEN. ► Umbilicus and Sides. [230.] ► Tearing about the umbilicus, as after eating green fruit, or as after taking cold (second day), [4a] . ► Cutting pain about the umbilicus, at 7 P.M. (second day), [1] . ► Pressive pain about the navel on stooping (first day), [7] . ► Burning and griping about the navel, extending into the small of the back (first day), [7] . ► Burning about the navel, which extended into the small of the back and changed to a sticking pain, [7a] . ► Burning about the navel while urinating, [7b] . ► Pressure outward in the flanks (third day), [4d] . ► Cutting pain extending from the flanks into the abdomen (fourth day), [7c] . ► Stitches in the right flank, aggravated by deep breathing; this pain afterwards extended to the pit of the stomach (first day), [7] . ► Very acute sticking pain in the left flank, aggravated by deep breathing, not affected by coughing (first day), [7] . [240.] ► Sticking in the left flank, extending towards the middle of the abdomen (third day), [7c] . ► Dull short stitches in both flanks (first day), [4d] . ► General Abdomen. ► Tympanitic distension of the abdomen (fourth day), [7c] . ► Tense distension of the abdomen after dinner (third day), [4d] . ► Circumscribed distension of the abdomen (first day), [7c] . ► Distension of the abdomen, with painful pressing down towards the anus, as from flatulence (after one hour), [4d] . ► Fulness and distension of the upper abdomen, in the afternoon, with tightness of the breath on ascending steps (first day), [4a] . ► Much movement of flatus in the abdomen (second day), [4b] . ► Profuse emission of flatus (first night), [4a] . ► Frequent emission of flatus, with general relief (first day), [4a] . [250.] ► Frequent emission of most offensive flatus, preceded by rumbling in the abdomen, with faintness and sensitiveness; during the emission of flatus there escapes a little watery fæces (frequently repeated), (first day), [4d] . ► Excessive accumulation of intestinal gas (first day), [4d] . ► Great accumulation of flatus in the bowels (second day), [4d] . ► Very frequent emission of extremely offensive flatus (fourth day), [7c] . ► Emission of flatus, accompanied by moisture in the anus (second day), [4d] . ► Emission of a large amount of exceedingly offensive flatus, with escape of thin fæces (second day), [4d] . ► Accumulation of gas in the bowels and much noise in the abdomen (second day), [4b] . ► Constant movements in the abdomen, as in diarrha, with emission of much offensive flatus, and at times a somewhat pasty stool (second day), [4b] . ► Much rumbling in the bowels, with profuse emission of flatus, [3] . ► Constant rumbling gurgling in the abdomen, at times accompanied with slight griping (second day), [4d] . [260.] ► Fulness and heaviness in the abdomen, causing an inclination to take a deep breath (first day), [4a] . ► Colic, as from a purge, or as after taking cold, followed by pressure upon the rectum, as though he should go to stool (second day), [4a] . ► Spasmodic laborlike drawing pains from the abdomen into the small of the back, as if the menses would come on (first day), [7] . ► Painfulness of the abdomen to touch, especially about the navel (first day), [7] . ► Drawing pain in the abdomen (third day), [4b] . ► Sensitiveness and a bruised pain in the upper abdomen (second day), [4d] . ► Pain in the abdomen, as from incarcerated flatus, with frequent emission of flatus, followed by great relief (first day), [7c] . ► Dragging and pressure as if the menses would come on (sixth day after menstruation), (first day), [7] . ► Pressing pain in the groins (first day), [7] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Pressure upon the rectum, which seems to force out the anus (third day), [4d] . [270.] ► Frequent urging pressure in the rectum, obliging her to go to stool, followed by emission of only flatus, and constant tenesmus, so that she did not rise from stool for a long time (third day), [5] . ► Dragging, as from a weight, in the rectum (second day), [4b] . ► Stitches in the rectum, extending in a long line down towards the anus (second day), [4d] . ► Sudden and urgent desire for stool while urinating so that he could scarcely reach the closet though previously he had had no inclination thereto, in the evening (second day), [4a] . ► Urgent desire for stool, and evacuation of a large quantity of very offensive mushy fæces, preceded by rumbling and emission of flatus (second day), [4b] . ► Urgent desire for stool, with diarrha-like evacuation, followed by movement in the abdomen as after a purge; during the course of the forenoon, there were two pasty stools with urgent desire; frequent rumbling and gurgling in the intestines (second day), [5] . ► Repeated ineffectual desire for stool (soon, third day), [4c] . ► Renewed desire for stool soon after an evacuation, which was accomplished only gradually and not without difficulty (third day), [4d] . ► Fruitless efforts at stool (first day), [4a] . ► Frequent desire for stool, with repeated but scanty and pasty evacuation, [3] . [280.] ► Absence of the usual desire for the morning stool (second day), [1] . STOOL. ► Diarrha. ► Diarrha, preceded by much movement of flatus in the abdomen (third day), [5] . ► Stool unusually early in the morning, it was softer than usual; this was followed after an hour by a second papescent stool, preceded by pressure in the rectum, and followed by slight tenesmus (second day), [4] . ► Stool mushy, unsatisfactory, after breakfast, followed after half an hour by renewed inclination, which disappeared after a difficult evacuation of flatus and eructations (third day), [4a] . ► A second pasty and scanty evacuation two hours after the first (third day), [4a] . ► Pasty stool, preceded by diarrha-like pain in the abdomen (third day), [4b] . ► Stool unusually early, scanty, consisting almost only of very offensive water, accompanied by pressure (second day), [4b] . ► Scanty pasty evacuation of the bowels, which though there had been a stool the evening previous, was seated low down in the rectum (second day), [4b] . ► Stool delayed, the first part hard, the latter soft and thin (second day), [4a] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Bladder and Urethra. ► Constant, almost painful pressure upon the bladder and urethra, obliging constant micturition; the evacuation of only a little dark orange-yellow urine; the evacuation of which caused a pain, at times when urinating and when not there was a sensation as if a sharp body were sticking in the urethra, [4c] . [290.] ► Dragging and forcing in the urethra, [4c] . ► Broad stitches in the middle of the urethra, at intervals, [3c] . ► Sudden piercing stitches through the urethra, especially towards the root of the penis (second day), [4a] . ► Micturition and Urine. ► Frequent and profuse micturition (first day), [7] . ► Micturition very early in the morning, with delayed evacuation and swollen feeling of the urethra; this was followed by three evacuations of considerable urine, at short intervals, the evacuation was gradual, and only after waiting a long time (third day), [4a] . ► Micturition more infrequent than in health; the urine passed in small quantity, and micturition was followed by a swollen sensation in the urethra; the pressure to urinate remained for a short time after a complete evacuation (third day), [4] . ► Wetting the bed (second day), [6] . ► Frequent copious evacuation of pale, odorless urine, in the afternoon (second day), [4] . ► Enuresis twice towards morning, while in a stupid slumber (first night), [6] . ► The urine seemed more copious, and was evacuated more frequently than usual during the forepart of the proving, [3] . [300.] ► Evacuation of urine delayed, with a feeling as if the whole course of the urethra were swollen (second day), [4a] . ► Frequent emission of clear urine, in a larger amount than usual (third day), [4d] . ► Pale and watery urine was frequently and copiously passed, during the forenoon, [4d] . ► Discharge of scanty dark brownish-red urine, of a penetrating odor, associated with soreness of the urethra (fourth day), [4d] . ► Much slimy sediment in the urine (second day), [5] . ► While urinating the child cried, since the urine made the external genitals sore on account of the eruption (second day), [6] .
